summary: No-ship: the new workflow prefix path injects an unsanitized workflow name directly into the operative prompt, so a crafted workflow filename can alter routed-turn instructions; the added tests also stop short of exercising the actual `gateway-pty` prefixing path.

findings.title: Workflow names are interpolated into the operative prompt without sanitization
findings.recommendation: Treat the workflow identifier as untrusted input. Sanitize or encode `route.target.workflow`/`targetId` before embedding it in prompt text and markers (for example, reject control characters/backticks/newlines, or quote a normalized identifier and keep the human-readable name out of the instruction body). This matters because workflow names come straight from filesystem basenames in `src/lib/workflows-scan.ts`.

findings.title: The new tests do not verify the actual routed-turn prefix injection in `gateway-pty`
findings.recommendation: Add a turn-level test around the `gateway-pty` routed path that asserts a `type:"workflow"` route sends `pre.annotation + workflowTurnPrefix + message`, and that non-workflow routes still send only `pre.annotation + message`. The current helper-only tests would still pass if `runRoutedTurn()` stopped prepending the prefix or started prepending it for plain turns.
